draft:
Hi Priya,

Thursday works — how about 2:00pm Pacific? I'll come with the launch sequencing notes so we can decide quickly.

— me

Hi Marcus,

Thanks for flagging this — sorry it's blocking your board prep. Looking into it now; the 500 on CSV export started after a deploy this morning. Two things:

1. As an immediate workaround, the same data is exportable via the /api/v1/metrics endpoint with format=csv (docs: link). If that doesn't work for you in the next hour, ping me and I'll send a one-off CSV directly.

2. We'll have the dashboard export fixed today and I'll confirm here once it's deployed.

Will follow up shortly.

— support
yeah i'm in — sign me up. what time does it start?

Hi Anya,

Welcome back! Tomorrow 2pm or 3pm Pacific would work for me. Nothing actively blocked but I'd like to walk through where we landed on the Q3 plan while you were out, plus a couple of staffing questions.

— me
Hi Dad — Saturday 10 your time works. I'll call you. Love to mom. ❤️

Hi Liang,

The timestamp header IS included in the signed payload — we use the Stripe-style scheme: signed_payload = timestamp + '.' + raw_body, then HMAC-SHA256 with your endpoint secret. The signature header carries both v1=<sig> and t=<timestamp>.

We'll update the docs to be explicit. Let me know if you hit anything else before Friday.

— me

Hi Daniela,

Thanks for the detailed report — having the crash log and the 100% repro is enormously helpful. I've filed BUG-1842 and routed it to mobile. We're prioritising it for the next beta build (this week).

While we work on the fix, two workarounds for unblocking your testing:

1. Files <50MB succeed reliably; could you continue scenario testing with smaller assets?
2. If you absolutely need a >50MB upload tested, the staging web app (link below) accepts the same files and we can verify end-to-end behavior there.

I'll update you as soon as we have a build with the fix.

— support

Hi Maya,

Thanks for reaching out — would love to grab coffee. May 7 mornings or May 8 afternoon work best for me. Flexible on neighborhood; happy to come to wherever's convenient for you.

— me

Hi Iris,

Q2 OKR update for the platform team:

On track for both KR1 (P95 latency under 250ms — currently 235ms after the caching changes shipped Apr 18) and KR2 (zero security-critical incidents — clean for the quarter so far). Behind on KR3 (40% reduction in on-call alerts) — we're at ~22% reduction; the remaining gain needs the new alert routing which now slips to mid-May.

Main risk: storage migration is one engineer light. Hana's plan addresses it but will compress the timeline. I'll have a more concrete read by next week.

— me
Acknowledged. Reviewing Dependabot PR #2350 now and will get it merged today after CI is green. Will confirm in this thread once shipped.

Hi Mom — calling tonight! Quick take: I'd lean toward the French place downtown — better for a long sit-down and dad always loved the duck there. The steakhouse is fine but louder. Open to ideas if you have one. Talk in a few hours. ❤️

Hi Priya,

May 28 for soft launch is doable assuming the migration finishes cleanly the week before. There's a small risk on the auth-flow refactor — if that slips we're looking at June 4. I'd rather not commit firm to May 28 today; can I confirm by Monday once we've passed the staging gate? That still gives you 5 days before your Friday lock.

— me

Hi People Ops,

Confirming I've completed benefits enrollment as of today. Same elections as last year.

— me

Hi Helena,

Thanks for the detailed write-up — this is exactly the level of specificity that helps us prioritise. Quick honest answer:

Bulk webhook retry from the dashboard is on the roadmap but not currently scheduled for this quarter (Q2). The shape you described (time-range filter, ack-status filter, confirm dialog) matches what we'd build, so I'd want to use this thread as the canonical spec.

What I can commit to today:
1. I'll get this prioritised in our Q3 planning session next month and write back to you with a concrete timeline by mid-May.
2. As a partial unblock now, our /api/v1/webhooks/retry endpoint accepts a time range and an ack-status filter — same logic the eventual UI would call. Happy to share an example script your team could run instead of writing fresh ones each time.

Would a 30-min call next week to walk through the use case still be useful, or does the above cover it?

— me

Hi David,

Will skim and react by Thursday EOD. On staffing for the migration: my early take is yes, we're under-resourced — we have 2 ICs covering what was supposed to be a 3-IC scope, and one of them is 50% on the security audit through May. I'll write this up properly in the doc rather than fragmenting the discussion here.

— me
